name: docx2txt (DOCX Text Extraction)

performance.notes: Simple XML parsing of DOCX ZIP; fast for text extraction

agent_readiness.notes: Extract plain text from .docx files. import docx2txt. text = docx2txt.process('file.docx'). → returns string with all text. Also extracts images: docx2txt.process('file.docx', '/path/to/images/'). DOCX is ZIP-based XML — docx2txt unpacks and parses document.xml. No formatting preserved (no bold, italic, tables as structure). python-docx for full document manipulation with formatting. mammoth for docx→HTML with style mapping. Minimal dependencies (none beyond stdlib).

gotchas: No formatting — plain text only, no tables, no styles
Headers and footers may not be extracted
python-docx is more maintained and feature-complete
mammoth better for DOCX → HTML with structure preservation
best_when: Quick plain text extraction from DOCX for NLP/LLM pipelines
Batch text extraction from Word documents
Simple search indexing of DOCX content
avoid_when: Need to preserve formatting, tables, or document structure
Need to write/modify DOCX files (use python-docx)
summary: docx2txt extracts plain text from .docx files. import docx2txt; text = docx2txt.process('file.docx'). Images: docx2txt.process('file.docx', './img_dir/'). Zero dependencies (stdlib only). MIT. Stalled since 2017. Returns raw text without formatting. Best for quick NLP/LLM text extraction from Word docs. Use python-docx for manipulation, mammoth for HTML conversion.
